title: BlackBerry Q3 Earnings Preview: What to Expect
text: BlackBerry was set to report Q3 earnings. Analysts expected BlackBerry's earnings to drop by 60% to $0.02 per share while revenue would jump by 22% to $275.73 million for the third quarter. The bottom line was likely to be hurt by higher costs and expenses while the top line would be benefited by higher billings as well as licensing revenue. The top-line growth was predicted to be backed by possible double-digit growth in billings. Additionally, margins were anticipated to increase driven primarily by Licensing and Cylance despite declines in Other and Internet of Things. The company could experience softness in its enterprise software and services business due to the retooling of its salesforce. However, in the Licensing business, the second half of fiscal 2020 was likely to be stronger than the first half. The majority of analysts recommended a hold rating with an average price target of $7.65.
